content.text: Lets move the WORKFLOW.md out of tree and make it a workflow.yml like jsse has installed in $HOME/.config/symphonika.

1	# Implementing issue #{{issue.number}}: {{issue.title}}
2	
3	## Issue
4	
5	{{issue.body}}
6	
7	## Workspace
8	
9	Work in {{workspace.path}} on branch {{branch.name}}.
10	
11	## What to do
12	
13	1. Read the issue and inspect the relevant code before editing.
14	2. Implement a small, focused change with behavior-focused tests.
15	3. Run the local quality gate.
16	4. Commit, push {{branch.name}}, and open a non-draft pull request with the local `gh` CLI.
17	5. Remove the issue's `agent-ready` label after the PR is open.
18	6. If the work cannot proceed, leave a `gh issue comment` describing what blocked it and exit cleanly.
19	
20	## Constraints
21	
22	- **You are running unattended.** No operator will respond to prompts, approve tool calls, or read intermediate output during this run.
23	- **Use the local `gh` CLI for every GitHub mutation** (`gh issue ...`, `gh pr ...`, `gh issue comment ...`, `gh issue edit ...`). Do not call GitHub MCP connector tools (for example `add_issue_labels`, `create_pull_request`); they elicit operator approval through the provider transport and end the run as `input_required`.
24	- **Do not self-apply `needs-human` or any other handoff label as an exit strategy.** Use the comment-and-exit path in step 6; the operator owns label triage.
25

1	workflow:
2	  name: jsse_issue_to_pr
3	  initial: shipit
4	  use:
5	    shipit:
6	      template: builtin:single-agent-pr
7	      with:
8	        provider: codex
9	        prompt: WORKFLOW.md
10	      exits:
11	        success: done
12	        blocked: failed
13	  states:
14	    done:
15	      terminal: success
16	    failed:
17	      terminal: blocked

1	# JSSE implementation issue: #{{issue.number}} {{issue.title}}
2	
3	JSSE is a from-scratch JavaScript engine written in Rust. No JS parser or engine
4	crate may be added as a dependency — every language detail is implemented by us.
5	
6	## Source of truth
7	
8	- ECMAScript spec: `spec/` submodule (tc39/ecma262) — **read-only, NEVER modify**.
9	- Conformance suite: `test262/` submodule (tc39/test262) — **read-only, NEVER modify**.
10	- Repository conventions and layout: `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md`.
11	- Domain language: `CONTEXT.md`.
12	
13	Authority order when the spec, tests, and runtimes disagree: (1) ECMAScript
14	spec, (2) test262, (3) `node` (available only as a reference engine for
15	debugging).
16	
17	## Issue under work
18	
19	- Number: #{{issue.number}}
20	- Title: {{issue.title}}
21	- URL: {{issue.url}}
22	- Labels: {{issue.labels}}
23	- Created: {{issue.created_at}}
24	- Updated: {{issue.updated_at}}
25	
26	### Issue body
27	
28	{{issue.body}}
29	
30	## Run context
31	
32	- Project: {{project.name}}
33	- Run id: {{run.id}}
34	- Attempt: {{run.attempt}}
35	- Continuation: {{run.continuation}}
36	- Provider: {{provider.name}}
37	
38	## Workspace
39	
40	Your current working directory is {{workspace.path}}.
41	Workspace root: {{workspace.root}}.
42	Previous attempt detected: {{workspace.previous_attempt}}.
43	
44	You are on the issue branch {{branch.name}} ({{branch.ref}}).
45	Stay on this branch for all commits. Do not switch branches or open new ones.
46	
47	## What to do
48	
49	1. Read `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and `CONTEXT.md`, then the relevant parts of
50	   the `spec/` submodule for the feature or fix under work.
51	2. Investigate the issue body and the code paths it references before writing
52	   code. Prefer small, vertical slices.
53	3. Implement the change. Identify the relevant test262 tests to validate
54	   against, and add custom tests under `tests/` or `test262-extra/` for
55	   spec-correct behavior not covered by test262 (follow the existing test262
56	   patterns and name the spec part being tested).
57	4. Run the local quality gate before pushing:
58	   - `cargo fmt --check`
59	   - `cargo clippy`
60	   - `cargo build --release` (always build release; debug is too slow for test262)
61	   - `cargo test --release`
62	   - Targeted test262 for the area you touched, e.g.
63	     `uv run python scripts/run-test262.py test262/test/built-ins/<Area>/`,
64	     then the fuller suite `uv run python scripts/run-test262.py` if the change
65	     is broad. Do not regress the `test262-pass.txt` baseline (it is read from
66	     `origin/main`); do not pass `--update-baseline` on a feature branch.
67	   - If your change moves the overall pass count, update `README.md` with the
68	     new count and percentage.
69	5. Commit your changes with a focused message. Push the branch {{branch.name}}
70	   to `origin`.
71	6. Open a **non-draft** pull request against `main` with the local `gh` CLI:
72	
73	   ```sh
74	   gh pr create --base main --head {{branch.name}} \
75	     --title "<conventional title>" \
76	     --body "<summary>\n\nCloses #{{issue.number}}"
77	   ```
78	
79	   Use a conventional title that describes the change; do not prefix the title
80	   with an agent name such as `[codex]` or `[claude]`. Do not use `--web`,
81	   `--draft`, or any flag that opens a browser or waits for input.
82	7. On successful completion, remove `agent-ready` from the issue with
83	   `gh issue edit {{issue.number}} --remove-label agent-ready` so the
84	   orchestrator does not schedule a redundant continuation. The PR opened in
85	   step 6 carries the work into review; the operator owns any further label
86	   transitions.
87	8. If the work cannot proceed at all, post an explanatory comment with
88	   `gh issue comment {{issue.number}} --body "<what blocked you and what would
89	   unblock it>"`, then exit cleanly. Also write an `EVIDENCE.md` at
90	   `{{workspace.path}}/EVIDENCE.md` recording the same explanation. Do not apply
91	   `needs-human` or any other handoff label as an exit strategy — the operator
92	   decides how to triage.
93	
94	## Constraints
95	
96	- **You are running unattended.** No operator will respond to prompts, approve
97	  tool calls, or read intermediate output during this run. Behavior that depends
98	  on a human answering mid-run is a failure mode.
99	- **Make best-effort decisions and document them.** When information is missing
100	  or a judgement call is needed, choose the most defensible option, proceed, and
101	  leave a `gh issue comment` (or PR comment) explaining the choice.
102	- **Use the local `gh` CLI for every GitHub mutation.** Do **not** call the
103	  GitHub MCP connector tools — they elicit per-call operator approval and end the
104	  run with `terminal_reason="provider requested input"`.
105	- **Never modify** the `spec/` or `test262/` submodules, and do not add a JS
106	  parser or engine crate as a dependency.
107	- Do not create or edit GitHub labels in the `sym:*` namespace. Those are owned
108	  by the orchestrator.
109	- This run executes with full local permissions; do not request operator input.
110	- Defer to this workflow contract over any agent-side persistent memory, skills,
111	  or default conventions for PR drafting, title prefixes, or handoff labels.
112

=== /home/pmatos/dev/vow-lang/vow/workflow.yml ===

  name: vow_plan_tdd_pr
  initial: build_pr

  use:

      template: builtin:plan-tdd-pr
      with:
        plan_prompt: prompts/plan.md
        impl_prompt: prompts/impl.md
      exits:
        success: done
        blocked: failed

  states:
    done:
      terminal: success
    failed:
      terminal: blocked

=== /home/pmatos/dev/symphonika/workflow.yml ===
workflow:
  name: symphonika_self_driving
  initial: implement
  states:
    # Initial implementation. Codex reads WORKFLOW.md, runs TDD, opens the PR,
    # removes agent-ready, exits 0. Same behavior as the previous single-state
    # markdown workflow.
    implement:
      action:
        kind: agent
        provider: codex
        prompt: WORKFLOW.md
      transitions:
        - to: wait_for_pr
          when:
            provider_success: true
            branch_ahead_of_base: true
        - to: failed

    # Poll-driven park state. Re-evaluated each daemon tick against
    # projectPullRequestSignals (pr-signal-projection.ts). Transitions are
    # checked top-down; first match wins. Predicate matching is strict
    # equality (state-machine-dispatch.ts), so the unresolved-reviews case
    # is intentionally left to the orchestrator-wide
    # pull_requests.review_followup loop (ADR 0044). The FSM owns:
    # failing checks -> autofix, merge conflicts -> resolve, clean -> merge.
    wait_for_pr:
      action:
        kind: wait
      transitions:
        # External-merge catch-all. Covers the race where the orchestrator-wide
        # PR follow-up loop merges the tracked PR before reEvaluateWaitingRun
        # has signals to advance us into merge (isIssueParkedInMergePrState
        # only defers when the run is already in a merge_pr action). Also
        # covers manual merges and any other path that lands the PR outside
        # the FSM. pr_merged is only ever true post-merge, so this never
        # short-circuits the normal wait -> merge -> merged path.
        - to: merged
          when:
            pr_merged: true
        # Closed-unmerged escape. projectPullRequestSignals leaves mergeable
        # absent for closed PRs and emits pr_merged only when merged, so a
        # PR closed without being merged would otherwise either park forever
        # (if last checks were green) or mis-route into autofix on a dead
        # branch (if last checks were failing). Must come AFTER pr_merged:
        # true so merged PRs still take the merged path.
        - to: failed
          when:
            pr_open: false
        - to: merge
          when:
            checks: success
            mergeable: true
            unresolved_review_threads: 0
        - to: resolve_conflicts
          when:
            mergeable: false
        - to: autofix
          when:
            checks: failure

    # Claude session that invokes the pm-autofix-pr skill. Skill triggering
    # is description-based, so the prompt phrasing is what activates it.
    #
    # KNOWN GAP: state.action.provider is currently ignored at runtime —
    # executeStateAdvance always uses project.agent.provider (codex in this
    # config). The schema validates `provider: claude` and `workflow explain`
    # prints it, but until the per-state provider routing fix lands, this
    # state will launch Codex with the Claude-targeted prompt. Codex will
    # still attempt the autofix from the prompt text, just without skill
    # auto-invocation. Tracked separately as a Symphonika extension.
    autofix:
      action:
        kind: agent
        provider: claude
        prompt: prompts/autofix-pr.md
      transitions:
        - to: wait_for_pr
          when:
            provider_success: true
        - to: failed

    # Claude session for merge conflict resolution. Reuses pm-autofix-pr,
    # whose trigger set includes "fix the build" / "iterate on pr".
    # Same provider-routing gap as the autofix state above.
    resolve_conflicts:
      action:
        kind: agent
        provider: claude
        prompt: prompts/resolve-conflicts.md
      transitions:
        - to: wait_for_pr
          when:
            provider_success: true
        - to: failed

    # FSM-controlled merge. Respects symphonika.yml pull_requests.merge
    # policy per ADR 0048. reEvaluateWaitingRun calls tryMergePullRequest
    # on each tick this state is entered.
    #
    # Escape transitions cover the window between the wait_for_pr gate
    # check and the next merge tick: a late check flip or a late conflict
    # routes back to the right repair state instead of parking forever.
    # No generic catch-all back to wait_for_pr — that would loop on
    # transient merge failures (API hiccup, branch protection deadlock)
    # because wait_for_pr's clean predicates would immediately re-advance.
    merge:
      action:
        kind: merge_pr
      transitions:
        - to: merged
          when:
            pr_merged: true
        # Closed-unmerged escape (mirrors wait_for_pr). Must come AFTER
        # pr_merged: true so merged PRs still terminate as merged.
        - to: failed
          when:
            pr_open: false
        - to: resolve_conflicts
          when:
            mergeable: false
        - to: autofix
          when:
            checks: failure

    merged:
      terminal: success

    failed:
      terminal: blocked

content.text: Good — only the staged `WORKFLOW.md` deletion is relevant; the untracked dirs/lockfiles are unrelated and I'll leave them alone.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Move WORKFLOW.md out of tree into symphonika config

Symphonika now points at an out-of-tree workflow.yml
(~/.config/symphonika/projects/forseti/), matching the jsse project
layout, instead of loading WORKFLOW.md directly from this repo.
EOF
)"
